channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
item.description: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The activity h...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 15-year-old detained over French govt agency data breach
item.description: French authorities have detained a 15-year-old suspected of selling data stolen in a cyberattack on France Titres (ANTS), the country's agency for issuing and managing administrative documents. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Story retracted
item.description: BleepingComputer initially published a story about a new data breach at Instructure. Shortly after publication, we determined that the information was incorrect and primarily based on outdated details...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: If AI's So Smart, Why Does It Keep Deleting Production Databases?
item.description: The issue isn't artificial intelligence, but rather an industry adding AI agent integrations into production environments before proper security testing....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of two cybercrime groups that are carrying out "rapid, high-impact attacks" operating almost within the confines of SaaS environments, while leaving minimal trace...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Criminal IP and Securonix ThreatQ Collaborate to Enhance Threat Intelligence Operations
item.description: Raw threat intel isn't enough without real-world context. Criminal IP has partnered with Securonix to integrate exposure-based intelligence into ThreatQ, automating analysis and speeding up investigat...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Target Asian Governments, NATO State, Journalists, and Activists
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new China-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defense sectors across South, East, and Southeast Asia, along with one European gove...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes Remote Desktop warnings displaying incorrectly
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing newly introduced Windows security warnings to display incorrectly when opening Remote Desktop (.rdp) files. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Security) Progress
item.description: Feeling creative? Have something to say about the last 20 years of cybersecurity? Our editors will award the best cybersecurity-related caption with a $20 gift card....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 20 Years in Cyber: Dark Reading Marks Milestone With Month of Special Coverage
item.description: On this day in 2006, Dark Reading went live. We have a celebration planned that spans our two decades of covering the industry, and you, dear readers, are invited....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft now lets admins choose pre-installed Store apps to uninstall
item.description: Microsoft has updated a Windows 11 in-box app removal policy introduced in October to include a dynamic list that lets IT admins choose which preinstalled Store apps to uninstall. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Top Five Sales Challenges Costing MSPs Cybersecurity Revenue
item.description: The managed security services market is projected to grow from $38.31 billion in 2025 to $69.16 billion by 2030[1], with cybersecurity being the fastest-growing sector[2]. Despite this opportunity, ma...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Windows 11 KB5083631 update released with 34 changes and fixes
item.description: Microsoft has released the KB5083631 optional cumulative update for Windows 11, which includes 34 changes, such as a new Xbox mode for Windows PCs, enhanced security and performance for batch files, a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Get 4-Year Sentences in BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the sentencing of two cybersecurity professionals to four years each in prison for their role in facilitating BlackCat ransomware attacks in ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
item.description: A new software supply chain attack campaign has been observed using sleeper packages as a conduit to subsequently push malicious payloads that enabled credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering, and S...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US ransomware negotiators get 4 years in prison over BlackCat attacks
item.description: Two former employees of cybersecurity incident response companies Sygnia and DigitalMint were sentenced to four years in prison each for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: TeamPCP Hits SAP Packages With 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Attack
item.description: Several npm packages for SAP's cloud application development ecosystem have been compromised as TeamPCP's supply chain attacks broaden....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Another AI-Assisted Software Scan Yields 9-Year-Old Linux Bug
item.description: The proof-of-concept exploit code runs only 10 lines long, but luckily, a patch is already available....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Anthropic's Mythos Has Landed: Here's What Comes Next for Cyber
item.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how the new AI model threatens to completely upend cybersecurity, and what industry leaders are telling the press....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Bluekit phishing service includes an AI assistant, 40 templates
item.description: A new phishing kit named Bluekit offers more than 40 templates targeting popular services and includes basic AI features for generating campaign drafts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Romanian leader of online swatting ring gets 4 years in prison
item.description: A Romanian national who led an online swatting ring that targeted more than 75 public officials, multiple journalists, and four religious institutions was sentenced to 4 years in federal prison. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI links cybercriminals to sharp surge in cargo theft attacks
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned the transportation and logistics industry of a sharp rise in cyber-enabled cargo theft, with estimated losses in the United States and Canada reac...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: PyTorch Lightning and Intercom-client Hit in Supply Chain Attacks to Steal Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aiki...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: April KB5083769 Windows 11 update causes backup software failures
item.description: The April 2026 KB5083769 security update breaks third-party backup applications from multiple vendors on systems running Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks ...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: What Happens in the First 24 Hours After a New Asset Goes Live
item.description: When a new asset goes live, attackers start scanning within minutes. Sprocket Security shows how automated attacks move from discovery to compromise in under 24 hours. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Linux ‘Copy Fail’ flaw gives hackers root on major distros
item.description: An exploit has been published for a local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed "Copy Fail" that impacts Linux kernels released since 2017, allowing an unprivileged local attacker to gain root per...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
item.description: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
